summary: AI-native power activation platform for data-center grid capacity.
editor_note: The interesting bet is that AI infrastructure is constrained by interconnection, not just GPUs. GridCARE turns grid utilization, congestion, outages, weather, and flexible-load dispatch into Power Finder, Activation, and Operations workflows. Unlike energy dashboards, it sells under-12-month time to power and 650 MW connection capacity evidence. The risk is whether utility partnerships scale city by city.

funding.lead_investor: Sutter Hill Ventures and John Doerr

product_evidence.claim: The official site presents Power Finder, Power Activation, and Power Operations as platform modules for data-center energy activation and delivery.
GridCARE publishes evidence for under-12-month activated power, 650 MW of identified New York connection capacity, and hundreds of megawatts unlocked for AI infrastructure.

market_context.primary_user: AI data-center developers, utilities, and energy operators trying to connect large flexible loads faster.
market_context.differentiation: GridCARE applies AI and grid modeling to latent capacity and flexible interconnection rather than waiting for new transmission buildout.
market_context.why_now: The May 2026 Series A comes as power availability becomes a binding constraint for AI data centers and local grid planning.

risks.claim: The open question is whether utility-by-utility approvals and local grid constraints let the model scale beyond early marquee projects.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
